title: Certificate of Formation — Nonprofit Corporation
disclaimer: Self-help document service — NOT legal or tax advice. Nonprofit formation and any later federal tax-exemption are separate steps with their own requirements; verify with the Texas Secretary of State and the IRS and consult a licensed professional. You file with the Texas SOS yourself.

fields.name: entity_name
fields.label: Nonprofit corporation name
fields.help: Must be distinguishable from existing TX entities. Check availability with the TX SOS.

fields.name: registered_agent_name
fields.label: Registered agent name
fields.help: Consenting registered agent with a Texas address. See the registered-agent template.

fields.name: registered_office_address
fields.label: Registered office address (TX)
fields.help: Physical Texas street address (not a PO box).

fields.name: has_members
fields.label: Will the corporation have members?
fields.help: Texas nonprofit corporations may or may not have members; the structure affects governance.

fields.name: directors
fields.label: Initial directors
fields.help: Names and addresses of the initial board of directors (TX generally requires at least three).

fields.name: organizer_name
fields.label: Organizer name
fields.help: The person forming the corporation (signs the certificate).

fields.name: purpose
fields.label: Purpose statement
fields.help: Statement of the nonprofit purpose. NOTE: if you intend to seek 501(c)(3) status later, the IRS generally requires specific purpose-limitation and dissolution language — verify the federal requirements separately (see the 1023 template).

fields.name: includes_501c3_language
fields.label: Include 501(c)(3) purpose/dissolution language?
fields.help: Whether the user is including the IRS-required clauses for a planned federal exemption. The USER decides; verify against current IRS guidance.
